componentDoc.name: button
componentDoc.description: An interactive element that triggers an action when activated. Buttons communicate what will happen when the user interacts with them.
componentDoc.summary: An interactive element that triggers an action when activated.

whenToUse.description: When the user needs to trigger an action such as submitting a form, saving data, or opening a dialog.
When a destructive or irreversible action needs to be initiated, such as deleting a record. Pair with a confirmation dialog.

whenNotToUse.description: When the action navigates the user to a different page or URL.

alternative.name: link
alternative.rationale: Links carry native navigation semantics. Screen readers announce them as links, and browsers support standard navigation behaviors such as open-in-new-tab.

whenNotToUse.description: When the only content is an icon with no visible text label.

alternative.name: icon-button
alternative.rationale: Icon buttons enforce an aria-label requirement and apply size adjustments for icon-only touch targets.

guidelines.guidance: Limit each surface to one primary button.
guidelines.rationale: Multiple primary buttons dilute visual hierarchy. When everything is emphasized, nothing is.

guidelines.guidance: Use the loading state instead of disabling the button during asynchronous operations.
guidelines.rationale: A disabled button gives no feedback that an action is in progress. The loading state communicates that the action was registered and the system is working.

guidelines.guidance: When using an icon-only button, provide an aria-label that describes the action.
guidelines.rationale: Screen readers announce button content as the accessible name. Without visible text, there is no accessible name.

guidelines.guidance: Use a verb or verb phrase that describes the action the button performs. Two words maximum.
guidelines.rationale: Action-oriented labels set clear expectations about what will happen on activation. Short labels prevent truncation.

ariaAttributes.description: Set to true when the button is non-interactive. Preferred over the HTML disabled attribute when the button should remain focusable.
Provides an accessible name for icon-only buttons.

accessibility.screenReaderBehavior: Announced as '[label], button'. When disabled via aria-disabled, announced as '[label], button, dimmed'.
accessibility.focusManagement: The button participates in the normal tab order. It does not trap or redirect focus.
